display_name: Western + Central Europe multi-era geometry + politics v1

priority_region.label: Western and Central Europe
priority_region.notes: M20 multi-region pack: composes WE + CE era-geometry packs for 1444, 1836, and 1936.

eras.notes: EU-leaning start: WE (Cologne split, Flanders) + CE (Prague split, Pannonia reshape, soft Habsburg/Ottoman/Polish bands).
Victoria-leaning start: WE (Belgian independence) + CE (Austrian Empire / Galicia / Hungarian lands soft geometry).
HOI-leaning interwar start: WE (Maginot / Rhineland) + CE (Sudeten / Anschluss / Polish corridor soft geometry).

region_quality_matrix.label: Europe elevated theaters (outside WE/CE hard overrides)

migration_notes.summary: Scaffold province IDs are the stable cross-era join key. Each era composes WE + CE era-geometry packs (applied in that order); use lineage.json to migrate. Politics are scenario overlays rebuilt per era.
migration_notes.lineage_strategy: Composed packs emit a merged lineage map. Do not assume era IDs match across 1444/1836/1936 or across WE vs CE packs. Cross-era analytics should join on scaffold_province_id.
consumer_guidance: Pin game saves and mod data to era province IDs when consuming a period-geometry layer.
Use lineage.json / lineage.csv under eras/<era>/geometry/ to migrate scaffold-only packs.
Rebuild ownership tables after geometry revisions — politics do not rewrite polygons.
Read region_quality_matrix.json: period geometry covers Western Europe and Central Europe only.
breaking_changes: we-1444-v1 splits sample_de_rhineland into era_de_cologne + era_de_rhineland_residual.
ce-1444-v1 splits sample_cz_bohemia into era_cz_prague + era_cz_bohemia_residual.
do_not_claim: identical province IDs across all three eras
Paradox-grade HRE / Bohemian Crown / Austrian Empire / interwar microborders

source_notes: M20 multi-region packaging: composes M15/M16 WE packs with new CE era-geometry packs.
Geometry packs are priority-region scoped; politics remain curated overlays on open geodata.
do_not_claim: Paradox-grade completeness for any single era or region
legal historical frontiers — soft hints are illustrative
